title: Minnesota Sues DOJ and DHS for Withholding Evidence in Three ICE Shooting Investigations
excerpt: Minnesota Attorney General Keith Ellison and Hennepin County Attorney Mary Moriarty sued the U.S. Departments of Justice and Homeland Security, alleging the federal government adopted a policy of 'categorically withholding evidence' to shield ICE officers from scrutiny in the shootings of Renee Good, Alex Pretti, and Julio Cesar Sosa-Celis.
content:
On March 24, 2026, Minnesota officials filed a federal lawsuit against the DOJ and DHS seeking access to evidence from three shootings by federal officers during Operation Metro Surge, including the fatal killings of Renee Good and Alex Pretti.

The lawsuit alleged the federal government 'reneged on its promise to cooperate' with state investigations and had 'adopted a policy of categorically withholding evidence' — a practice Moriarty called 'unprecedented and alarming.'

  • State officials said federal agents had blocked the Minnesota Bureau of Criminal Apprehension from accessing the Pretti crime scene and had ignored formal requests for evidence under 'Touhy' regulations.
  • Evidence withheld included Pretti's cell phone, Good's vehicle (described as 'shrink-wrapped in an FBI warehouse and never examined'), and personnel and training records for agent Jonathan Ross.
  • Minnesota prosecutors noted they could not even answer basic questions — 'When was Jonathan Ross trained? What was he trained to do?' — because all personnel and training records were withheld.
  • The lawsuit added that the federal government's evidence-withholding precluded prosecutors from making charging decisions.
mediaPlaceholder: Press conference footage of Minnesota AG Ellison and County Attorney Moriarty announcing the lawsuit
imageAlt: Minnesota Sues DOJ and DHS for Withholding Evidence in Three ICE Shooting Investigations
